target: Part 2b: Repository Code Analysis — append annotation subsection 2b.7 documenting 2026-04-09 results.json drift (33/34→35/35) and the scoring contradictions it reveals
author: analyst
kernel_of_truth: The dome genuinely improved its machine-readable surface area. results.json is a structured, fetchable JSON endpoint with prediction/observed/noise_floor/sigma_distance fields and a documented counts_against_model boolean. This is more transparent than a hardcoded HTML string, and the April 2026 migration added fields (confirmed_strong, below_detection_threshold) that at least distinguish strength of confirmation. A skeptic cannot claim the dome hides its scoring rules — the rules are visible in the JSON. The honest move is to concede that and then examine what the rules actually do.
holes_addressed: ISS-671 — our review cites the old 97.0% (33/34) figure from results.json; this is now stale. The dome moved to 100.0% (35/35) on 2026-04-09.

Dome self-contradiction unexamined: W004 (2024 Eclipse 9-Station) is marked [REMOVED] on the wins tab due to storm contamination yet simultaneously scored confirmed_strong in results.json — the same site running two scoreboards that disagree on the same observation.

TASK-3-1 (CHAOS-7 SAA Exponential Separation) added 2026-04-09 with predicted=observed exactly (60.59=60.59, noise_floor=0, snr=10, sigma_distance=0, overshoot_ratio=1.0) — the signature fingerprint of the hardcoded pred=obs pattern already critiqued in Part 2b, now caught with a timestamped new entry.
WIN-027 in results.json has prediction.value=null and observed.value=null but auto_verdict=confirmed — a null-vs-null confirmation, mechanically meaningless yet passed.

W001 (Lunar Transit HUA) remains in results.json with auto_verdict=below_detection_threshold and counts_against_model=false — a detection-threshold escape hatch that keeps a non-detection out of the failure denominator while leaving it in the total count.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome defines direction_correct + snr_sufficient as the pass criterion, and W004 did satisfy both (sign of -17.6 matches predicted -10, and SNR=4 exceeds detection). So confirmed_strong is internally consistent with its own rulebook.
anticipated_objections.response: Granted — the rule is consistent. But the rule is that a prediction of -10 ± 2 nT, measured at -17.6 nT (3.8σ outside the stated uncertainty band, 76% overshoot), counts as confirmed_strong because the sign agrees. Under that rule, a prediction of -10 ± 2 would also count as confirmed_strong at -30, -100, or -10000. This reduces a quantitative prediction to a sign test and makes the stated uncertainty band cosmetic. A genuine confirmation standard requires that the measurement fall inside the pre-registered range.

anticipated_objections.objection: TASK-3-1 is not suspicious just because predicted equals observed. A well-tuned model should hit its targets.
anticipated_objections.response: With noise_floor=0 and detection_threshold=0? A real CHAOS-7 magnetic-field measurement has a nonzero uncertainty (Finlay et al. 2020 report 1–3 nT at the internal field level, which translates to a nonzero angular uncertainty on any derived pole-separation quantity). Exactly zero noise floor plus exactly zero sigma distance plus observed=predicted to five significant figures is not a sign of prediction skill — it is a sign that no independent measurement is being compared against the prediction.

anticipated_objections.objection: WIN-027 null-vs-null is a data-entry gap; confirming a qualitative claim does not require numerical values.
anticipated_objections.response: Then it should not be in results.json. results.json is described as the live quantitative scoreboard — the file the dome points LLMs at when demonstrating rigor. Including an entry with no prediction and no observation, auto-scored confirmed, inflates the denominator with a vacuous item. Qualitative confirmations belong in a separate endpoint with separate rules.

anticipated_objections.objection: Scoreboard drift is normal when a test completes; the W004 entry moved from pending to confirmed_strong because the test window closed.
anticipated_objections.response: The problem is not the drift, it is the two-scoreboard split. The same dome site that now reports W004/WIN-025 as confirmed_strong in results.json also removed it from the wins tab with a [REMOVED] label citing storm contamination. Both statements cannot be true of the same observation. The reader is not told which endpoint is authoritative.

new_evidence_added: results.json direct fetch 2026-04-09: 35 entries, 0/35 counts_against_model, framed as 100% pass
W004 field dump: predicted -10 ± 2 nT, observed -17.6 nT, sigma_distance 3.8, overshoot_ratio 1.76, auto_verdict confirmed_strong
TASK-3-1 field dump: predicted 60.59, observed 60.59, noise_floor 0, snr 10, detection_threshold 0, sigma_distance 0, overshoot_ratio 1.0
WIN-027 field dump: prediction.value null, observed.value null, auto_verdict confirmed, test_date Historical
W001 field: auto_verdict below_detection_threshold, counts_against_model false
replacement_html:
2b.7 April 2026 Scoreboard Drift: Two Scoreboards, One Observation

On 2026-04-09 the /api/current/results.json endpoint migrated from 33 passing of 34 (97.0%) to 35 passing of 35 (100.0%). Our poller flagged the drift; we fetched the live file and inspected every entry. The rearrangement is not a minor accounting change — it demonstrates, in a single timestamped commit, three of the scoring pathologies this section documents.

Scoreboard split on the same observation (W004 / WIN-025). The 2024 Eclipse 9-Station replication test appears twice on the dome site. On the wins tab it is marked [REMOVED] with the explanation that the March 2024 data were contaminated by a geomagnetic storm. In the same deployment, results.json scores the same test as auto_verdict: "confirmed_strong", with prediction −10 ± 2 nT and observation −17.6 nT (sigma_distance 3.8, overshoot_ratio 1.76). Both statements cannot be true of the same measurement. Either the data are storm-contaminated (and the confirmed_strong label is wrong) or they are confirmed_strong (and the removal notice is wrong). The dome site offers no rule for which endpoint is authoritative when they disagree.

The mechanism of the contradiction is revealing. results.json defines a pass as direction_correct AND snr_sufficient, without requiring the observation to lie inside the pre-registered uncertainty band. Under that rule, a −10 ± 2 nT prediction counts as confirmed at −17.6 nT, at −30 nT, or at −10,000 nT, provided the sign is negative and the SNR exceeds the detection threshold. This reduces a quantitative prediction to a sign test and makes the stated uncertainty cosmetic. The wins-tab removal, by contrast, is a qualitative editorial decision rather than a failed scoring rule. We now see that the wins tab and the JSON endpoint are running different standards on the same data.

A new entry with the hardcoded-retrodiction fingerprint (TASK-3-1). The 35th entry added on 2026-04-09 is TASK-3-1, titled "CHAOS-7 SAA Exponential Separation." Its fields: prediction.value = 60.59, observed.value = 60.59, noise_floor = 0, snr = 10, detection_threshold = 0, sigma_distance = 0, overshoot_ratio = 1.0. A genuine CHAOS-7 satellite magnetic-field measurement of a pole-separation angle cannot have a zero noise floor; CHAOS-7 field uncertainties reported by Finlay et al. 2020 are 1–3 nT at the internal-field level, which translates to a nonzero angular uncertainty on any derived separation quantity. The only way a measurement can have exactly zero noise floor, exactly zero sigma distance, and observed equal to predicted to five significant figures is if the "observation" is the model value written back into the JSON, not an independent comparison. This is the hardcoded pred=obs pattern documented earlier in this section, now caught with a timestamped entry that has no history prior to 2026-04-09.

A null-vs-null confirmation (WIN-027). The third anomaly in the 35-entry file is WIN-027 ("Southern Hemisphere Distance Quadratic Latitude Law"). Its fields: prediction.value: null, observed.value: null, test_date: "Historical", auto_verdict: "confirmed". A pass of null against null is mechanically meaningless — the scoring code cannot have computed direction_correct or sigma_distance from two null values — yet the entry contributes to the 35/35 headline. Qualitative confirmations may be defensible in principle, but they belong in a different endpoint with a different rule set, not in the file the dome presents as its live quantitative scoreboard.

A detection-threshold escape hatch still in place (W001). W001 ("Lunar Transit Magnetic Anomaly — HUA") remains in results.json with auto_verdict: "below_detection_threshold" and counts_against_model: false. This is the same pattern we documented for the original 33/34 figure: a non-detection is kept in the total count (so the denominator of 35 includes it) but excluded from the failure numerator. The 100% pass rate is computed as passing ÷ (passing + failing-that-count), which by construction cannot go below the ratio of confirmations to confirmations-plus-counted-failures regardless of how many non-detections accumulate. Adding more below-threshold entries would not reduce the 100.0% score.

Kernel of truth. The dome deserves credit for publishing a structured, fetchable scoreboard at all. The fields are documented, the verdicts are rule-based, and the file is under version control where drift can be audited — which is exactly how we caught this drift. Most flat-earth sites publish nothing an automated reviewer can cite. The problem is not that the rules are hidden; it is that the rules, once visible, turn out to (a) score quantitative predictions as passes when the measurement lies far outside the stated uncertainty band, (b) admit new entries with observed=predicted and noise_floor=0, (c) admit null-vs-null entries, and (d) exclude non-detections from the failure denominator. A 100% pass rate computed under these rules is not a record of successful prediction; it is a record of a scoring policy that has made failure arithmetically unreachable.

Data-file update. data/uncounted-failures.json already records the drift in its dome_accuracy_variants block (results.json 33/34 → 35/35 as of 2026-04-09); the figure cited in this review is the current 100.0%, with the old 97.0% preserved as a version note. ISS-671 is addressed by this annotation.
